title: Dorsey’s Twitter Departure Hints at Tech Moguls’ Restlessness
content: Jack Dorsey, who is stepping down after six years as Twitter’s chief executive, is one of the tech leaders who seem to have grown tired of managing their empires.
en_title: Dorsey’s Twitter Departure Hints at Tech Moguls’ Restlessness
en_content: Jack Dorsey, who is stepping down after six years as Twitter’s chief executive, is one of the tech leaders who seem to have grown tired of managing their empires.

title: Supply Chain Problems Have Small Retailers Gambling on Hoarding
content: Some independent stores ordered in bulk well in advance, and now are hoping they’re able to sell what they have.

en_title: Supply Chain Problems Have Small Retailers Gambling on Hoarding
en_content: Some independent stores ordered in bulk well in advance, and now are hoping they’re able to sell what they have.

title: In China, Tesla Is a Catfish, and Turns Auto Companies Into Sharks
content: The electric car company is helping Chinese companies become global players in the emerging industry, posing a competitive threat to traditional rivals.
en_title: In China, Tesla Is a Catfish, and Turns Auto Companies Into Sharks
en_content: The electric car company is helping Chinese companies become global players in the emerging industry, posing a competitive threat to traditional rivals.

title: Counterfeit Covid Masks Are Still Sold Everywhere, Despite Misleading Claims
content: Rising Covid cases have spurred a return to mask-wearing in the U.S. and overseas, at a time when flawed KN95s from China continue to dominate e-commerce sites.

org_facet: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

en_content: Rising Covid cases have spurred a return to mask-wearing in the U.S. and overseas, at a time when flawed KN95s from China continue to dominate e-commerce sites.

content: Ms. Holmes, the founder of the failed blood testing start-up Theranos, blamed Ramesh Balwani, the former No. 2 at the company.

title: Investors Snap Up Metaverse Real Estate in a Virtual Land Boom
content: Transactions for properties in digital realms are jumping, guided by the same principle in the physical world: location, location, location.

title: Union Vote at Amazon Warehouse in Alabama Is Overturned by Regional Labor Office
content: Amazon said in August that it intended to appeal to the national labor board if it did not prevail at the regional level, but it did not say Monday whether it would follow through.

org_facet: Retail, Wholesale and Department Store Union

en_title: Union Vote at Amazon Warehouse in Alabama Is Overturned by Regional Labor Office
en_content: Amazon said in August that it intended to appeal to the national labor board if it did not prevail at the regional level, but it did not say Monday whether it would follow through.

title: Millennials Confront High Inflation for the First Time
content: Americans born after 1980 have been wrestling with sharp price increases for the first time since they’ve been old enough to notice.

en_title: Millennials Confront High Inflation for the First Time
en_content: Americans born after 1980 have been wrestling with sharp price increases for the first time since they’ve been old enough to notice.

title: South African Company Nears License to Sell J. & J. Covid Shot Across Africa
content: The new agreement makes Aspen Pharmacare the first African company to market a Covid vaccine on the continent. But it stops short of allowing Aspen to make the ingredients in the vaccine.
en_title: South African Company Nears License to Sell J. & J. Covid Shot Across Africa
en_content: The new agreement makes Aspen Pharmacare the first African company to market a Covid vaccine on the continent. But it stops short of allowing Aspen to make the ingredients in the vaccine.

title: The head of Meta’s cryptocurrency efforts is leaving the company.
content: David Marcus had most recently spearheaded Meta’s push into a global digital currency.

title: Janet Yellen still supports a plan to make banks give the I.R.S. new customer data.
content: “I think it’s important that the I.R.S. have visibility into opaque income streams,” the Treasury secretary said.

title: Jim Warren, Early Influencer in Personal Computing, Dies at 85

title: Jerome Powell pivots, suggesting quicker reduction in economic help as high inflation persists.
content: The Federal Reserve could pull back economic support faster as inflation lasts, and its chair signaled that for now the Omicron variant is a “risk.”

title: Lululemon sues Peloton, accusing it of patent infringement.
content: Lululemon accused Peloton, the fitness company best known for its stationary bikes, of infringing on patents for a new line of leggings and sports bras.

title: ‘Our Money Has No Value’: Frustration Rises in Turkey at Lira Crisis
content: President Recep Tayyip Erdogan’s insistence on directing monetary policy and sticking with low interest rates is draining confidence, economists say.

title: Weak Recovery Leaves India’s Middle Class Anxious and Frugal
content: Covid-19 essentially robbed the country of more than a year of badly needed economic growth. That’s lost ground that cannot be regained quickly.
en_title: Weak Recovery Leaves India’s Middle Class Anxious and Frugal
en_content: Covid-19 essentially robbed the country of more than a year of badly needed economic growth. That’s lost ground that cannot be regained quickly.

title: Stocks fall as investors consider the possibility of a faster taper of bond purchases.

title: The Biden administration, under fire for supply chain woes, says stores will be fully stocked for the holidays.
content: President Biden has made a push to unclog ports, address trucker shortages and take other steps to alleviate the pressure created by consumers looking to buy couches, cars and electronics instead of eating out or going to theme parks.

title: Who Owns a Recipe? A Plagiarism Claim Has Cookbook Authors Asking.
content: U.S. copyright law protects all kinds of creative material, but recipe creators are mostly powerless in an age and a business that are all about sharing.

title: Clearview AI, a facial recognition company, is fined for breach of Britain’s privacy laws.
content: The Information Commissioner’s Office said it fined Clearview AI for failing to inform British residents that it was collecting billions of photos from sites including Facebook, Instagram and LinkedIn to build its facial recognition software.
en_title: Clearview AI, a facial recognition company, is fined for breach of Britain’s privacy laws.
en_content: The Information Commissioner’s Office said it fined Clearview AI for failing to inform British residents that it was collecting billions of photos from sites including Facebook, Instagram and LinkedIn to build its facial recognition software.
